message:
fix(csv): escape all dash-prefixed values and add newline to formula-injection set

\`escape_field\` already mitigated CSV formula injection by
prefixing dangerous-leading-char values with \`'\` and wrapping
in quotes, but the rule for dash was needlessly conditional: it
only escaped \`-\`-prefixed values whose second byte was an
ASCII digit (\`-3 items\`). Anything else starting with \`-\` was
passed through unescaped.

That is not safe against modern spreadsheets: Excel will
evaluate \`-func(...)\`, Google Sheets treats \`-cmd|...\` as a
negated invocation in some locales, and a handful of clients
interpret \`-reference!A1\` as a formula. All of those skip the
\"second char is a digit\" gate and slip through the old pattern.

Also add \`\\n\` to the dangerous-leading-char set. A cell whose
FIRST byte is a newline and whose second line starts with \`=1+1\`
renders as blank in most CSV readers but is re-parsed as a
formula by Excel after the ingesting tool strips or normalizes
the leading newline. Escape the whole value the same way we do
for the other whitespace triggers (\`\\t\`, \`\\r\`).

Changes in \`src/format/csv.rs\`:

- The dangerous-leading-char set becomes
  \`['=', '+', '-', '@', '\\t', '\\r', '\\n']\`. The ad-hoc
  \`value.starts_with('-') && value.len() > 1 &&
  value.as_bytes()[1].is_ascii_digit()\` branch is removed —
  any dash-prefixed value is now escaped.
- Doc-comment for \`escape_field\` is refreshed to list the full
  trigger set (\`=\`, \`+\`, \`-\`, \`@\`, \`\\t\`, \`\\r\`,
  \`\\n\`) so the contract is legible at the call site.

Test changes in the same file's \`mod tests\`:

- Replace the narrow
  \`test_escape_field_negative_number_prefix\` /
  \`test_escape_field_plain_dash_not_formula\` pair with a single
  \`test_escape_field_dash_prefix\` that asserts \`-3 items\`,
  \`-abc\`, and a bare \`-\` are ALL escaped to the quoted-
  single-quote form. The \"dash means don't escape\" behavior
  that the old test pinned was the vulnerability; deleting that
  test line is part of the fix.
- New \`test_escape_field_newline_prefix\` verifies
  \`\\n=1+1\` escapes to \`\"'\\n=1+1\"\`, pinning the added
  trigger char.

Pure hardening. Callers that pass literal \`-\`-prefixed text
through this function will now see those cells wrapped in quotes
with a leading \`'\`, which is exactly the existing treatment for
\`=\`-prefixed values.
